words.definitionEn: at an equal distance between two points; in the middle of a period of time
words.exampleEn: It's about halfway between London and Bristol.

words.definitionEn: an act of stopping the movement or progress of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: Work came to a halt when the machine broke down.

words.definitionEn: to stop; to make somebody/something stop
words.exampleEn: She walked towards him and then halted.

words.definitionEn: the amount of something that can be held in one hand
words.exampleEn: a handful of rice

words.definitionEn: the way that somebody deals with or treats a situation, a person, an animal, etc.
words.exampleEn: I was impressed by his handling of the affair.

words.definitionEn: easy to use or to do
words.exampleEn: a handy little tool

words.definitionEn: with a name that is not known or that is not made public
words.exampleEn: an anonymous donor

words.definitionEn: the act of annoying or worrying somebody by putting pressure on them or saying or doing unpleasant things to them
words.exampleEn: sexual/racial harassment

words.definitionEn: the machines and electronic parts in a computer or other electronic system
words.exampleEn: We supply computer hardware to businesses.

words.definitionEn: a state of peaceful existence and agreement
words.exampleEn: social/racial harmony

words.definitionEn: cruel, severe and unkind
words.exampleEn: The punishment was harsh and unfair.

words.definitionEn: the time of year when the crops are gathered in on a farm, etc.; the act of cutting and gathering crops
words.exampleEn: harvest time

words.definitionEn: to cut and gather a crop; to catch a number of animals or fish to eat
words.exampleEn: The nuts are ready to harvest in September.

words.definitionEn: a very strong feeling of dislike for somebody/something
words.exampleEn: He looked at me with intense hatred.

words.definitionEn: if the ghost of a dead person haunts a place, people say that they have seen it there
words.exampleEn: A headless rider haunts the country lanes.

words.definitionEn: something that can be dangerous or cause damage
words.exampleEn: a fire/safety hazard

words.definitionEn: if a feeling or an effect heightens, or something heightens it, it becomes stronger or increases
words.exampleEn: Tension has heightened after the recent bomb attack.

words.definitionEn: the history, traditions, buildings and objects that a country or society has had for many years and that are considered an important part of its character
words.exampleEn: Spain’s rich cultural heritage

words.definitionEn: a system, especially in a society or an organization, in which people are organized into different levels of importance from highest to lowest
words.exampleEn: the social/political hierarchy

words.definitionEn: receiving or involving a lot of attention and discussion in the media
words.exampleEn: a high-profile campaign

words.definitionEn: something that you say or do in an indirect way in order to show somebody what you are thinking
words.exampleEn: He gave a broad hint (= one that was obvious) that he was thinking of retiring.

words.definitionEn: to suggest something in an indirect way
words.exampleEn: hint at something, What are you hinting at?

words.definitionEn: the country where a person was born
words.exampleEn: Many refugees have been forced to flee their homeland.

words.definitionEn: to fasten or hang something on something else using a hook; to be fastened or hanging in this way
words.exampleEn: hook something + adv./prep., We hooked the trailer to the back of the car.

words.definitionEn: believing that something you want will happen
words.exampleEn: hopeful (that…), I feel hopeful that we'll find a suitable house very soon.

words.definitionEn: the furthest that you can see, where the sky seems to meet the land or the sea
words.exampleEn: The sun sank below the horizon.

words.definitionEn: a hard pointed part that grows, usually in pairs, on the heads of some animals, such as sheep and cows. Horns are often curved.
words.exampleEn: a large bull with curved horns

words.definitionEn: a person who is captured and held prisoner by a person or group, and who may be injured or killed if people do not do what the person or group is asking
words.exampleEn: Three children were taken hostage during the bank robbery.

words.definitionEn: aggressive or unfriendly and ready to argue or fight
words.exampleEn: The speaker got a hostile reception from the audience.

words.definitionEn: aggressive or unfriendly feelings or behaviour
words.exampleEn: There was a barely veiled hostility in her tone.

words.definitionEn: connected with helping people who are suffering and improving the conditions that they are living in
words.exampleEn: to provide humanitarian aid to the war zone

words.definitionEn: people in general
words.exampleEn: He was found guilty of crimes against humanity.

words.definitionEn: showing you do not think that you are as important as other people
words.exampleEn: Be humble enough to learn from your mistakes.

words.definitionEn: the process of showing, proving or recognizing who or what somebody/something is
words.exampleEn: The identification of the crash victims was a long and difficult task.

words.definitionEn: based on or connected with an ideology
words.exampleEn: ideological differences

words.definitionEn: a set of ideas that an economic or political system is based on
words.exampleEn: Marxist/capitalist ideology

words.definitionEn: a rude way to refer to somebody who you think is very stupid
words.exampleEn: When I lost my passport, I felt such an idiot.

words.definitionEn: a lack of knowledge or information about something
words.exampleEn: They fought a long battle against prejudice and ignorance.

words.definitionEn: language that produces pictures in the minds of people reading or listening
words.exampleEn: poetic imagery

words.definitionEn: extremely large or great
words.exampleEn: There is still an immense amount of work to be done.

words.definitionEn: likely to happen very soon
words.exampleEn: the imminent threat of invasion

words.definitionEn: the act of making something that has been officially decided start to happen or be used
words.exampleEn: the implementation of the new system

words.definitionEn: to put somebody in a prison or another place from which they cannot escape
words.exampleEn: be imprisoned (for something), They were imprisoned for possession of drugs.

words.definitionEn: the act of putting somebody in a prison or another place from which they cannot escape; the state of being there
words.exampleEn: to be sentenced to life imprisonment for murder

words.definitionEn: the fact of not being able to do something
words.exampleEn: the government’s inability to provide basic services

words.definitionEn: not enough; not good enough
words.exampleEn: inadequate supplies

words.definitionEn: not suitable or appropriate in a particular situation
words.exampleEn: inappropriate behaviour/language

words.definitionEn: the extent to which something happens or has an effect
words.exampleEn: an area with a high incidence of crime

words.definitionEn: wanting to do something
words.exampleEn: She was inclined to trust him.

words.definitionEn: the tools or other pieces of equipment that are needed for a particular activity or task
words.exampleEn: a piece of laboratory apparatus

words.definitionEn: the fact of including somebody/something; the fact of being included
words.exampleEn: His inclusion in the team is in doubt.

words.definitionEn: if you incur something unpleasant, you are in a situation in which you have to deal with it
words.exampleEn: She had incurred the wrath of her father by marrying without his consent.

words.definitionEn: a sign that shows you what something is like or how a situation is changing
words.exampleEn: The economic indicators are better than expected.

words.definitionEn: a sign that a system, society, etc. is very bad or very wrong
words.exampleEn: The poverty in our cities is a damning indictment of modern society.

words.definitionEn: coming from a particular place and having lived there for a long time before other people came there; relating to, belonging to or developed by these people
words.exampleEn: the indigenous peoples/languages of an area

words.definitionEn: to persuade or influence somebody to do something
words.exampleEn: Nothing would induce me to take the job.

words.definitionEn: to allow yourself to have or do something that you like, especially something that is considered bad for you
words.exampleEn: indulge in something, They went into town to indulge in some serious shopping.

words.definitionEn: the unfair difference between groups of people in society, when some have more wealth, status or opportunities than others
words.exampleEn: We need to tackle inequality of opportunity wherever we find it.

words.definitionEn: well known for being bad or evil
words.exampleEn: a general who was infamous for his brutality

words.definitionEn: a baby or very young child
words.exampleEn: a nursery for infants under two

words.definitionEn: to make a disease or an illness spread to a person, an animal or a plant
words.exampleEn: infect somebody/something (with something), It is not possible to infect another person through kissing.

words.definitionEn: to make somebody/something suffer something unpleasant
words.exampleEn: inflict something on/upon somebody/something, They inflicted a humiliating defeat on the home team.

words.definitionEn: having a lot of influence on somebody/something
words.exampleEn: a highly influential book

words.definitionEn: attractive or interesting
words.exampleEn: Spending the holidays in Britain wasn't a prospect that I found particularly appealing.

words.definitionEn: that is a basic or permanent part of somebody/something and that cannot be removed
words.exampleEn: the difficulties inherent in a study of this type

words.definitionEn: to prevent something from happening or make it happen more slowly or less frequently than normal
words.exampleEn: A lack of oxygen may inhibit brain development in the unborn child.

words.definitionEn: to make something begin
words.exampleEn: to initiate legal proceedings against somebody

words.definitionEn: to put a drug or other substance into a person’s or an animal’s body using a syringe
words.exampleEn: inject something (into yourself/somebody/something), Adrenaline was injected into the muscle.

words.definitionEn: an act of injecting somebody with a drug or other substance
words.exampleEn: to give somebody an injection

words.definitionEn: the fact of a situation being unfair and of people not being treated equally; an unfair act or an example of unfair treatment
words.exampleEn: We are committed to fighting against poverty and injustice.

words.definitionEn: one of the people living in an institution such as a prison or a psychiatric hospital
words.exampleEn: The jail has 500 inmates.

words.definitionEn: the act of putting something inside something else; a thing that is put inside something else
words.exampleEn: An examination is carried out before the insertion of the tube.

words.definitionEn: a person who knows a lot about a group or an organization, because they are part of it
words.exampleEn: The situation was described by one insider as ‘absolute chaos’.

words.definitionEn: physical desire for food
words.exampleEn: He suffered from headaches and loss of appetite.

words.definitionEn: to look closely at something/somebody, especially to check that everything is as it should be
words.exampleEn: inspect something/somebody, The teacher walked around inspecting their work.

words.definitionEn: an official visit to a school, factory, etc. in order to check that rules are being obeyed and that standards are acceptable
words.exampleEn: Regular inspections are carried out at the prison.

words.definitionEn: the process that takes place when somebody sees or hears something that causes them to have exciting new ideas or makes them want to create something, especially in art, music or literature
words.exampleEn: Dreams can be a rich source of inspiration for an artist.

words.definitionEn: to show your approval of somebody/something by clapping your hands (= hitting your open hands together several times)
words.exampleEn: He started to applaud and the others joined in.

words.definitionEn: a natural quality that makes people and animals tend to behave in a particular way using the knowledge and abilities that they were born with rather than thought or training
words.exampleEn: She did not seem to have any of the usual maternal instincts.

words.definitionEn: connected with a large important organization, for example a university or bank
words.exampleEn: institutional investors

words.definitionEn: to tell somebody to do something, especially in a formal or official way
words.exampleEn: instruct somebody to do something, The letter instructed him to report to headquarters immediately.

words.definitionEn: important in making something happen
words.exampleEn: The Conservation Trust performs an instrumental role in the protection of rural environments.

words.definitionEn: not large, strong or important enough for a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: insufficient time

words.definitionEn: a remark or an action that is said or done in order to offend somebody
words.exampleEn: The crowd were shouting insults at the police.

words.definitionEn: to say or do something that offends somebody
words.exampleEn: I have never been so insulted in my life!

words.definitionEn: complete and not damaged
words.exampleEn: Most of the house remains intact even after two hundred years.

words.definitionEn: the amount of food, drink, etc. that you take into your body
words.exampleEn: high fluid intake

words.definitionEn: being an essential part of something
words.exampleEn: Music is an integral part of the school's curriculum.

words.definitionEn: in which many different parts are closely connected and work successfully together
words.exampleEn: an integrated programme of patient care

words.definitionEn: that can be said to be true in the case of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: Give details of children where applicable (= if you have any).

words.definitionEn: the act or process of combining two or more things so that they work together
words.exampleEn: The aim is to promote closer economic integration.

words.definitionEn: the quality of being honest and having strong moral principles
words.exampleEn: personal/professional/artistic integrity

words.definitionEn: a person who is well educated and enjoys activities in which they have to think seriously about things
words.exampleEn: He was a leading intellectual of his day.

words.definitionEn: to increase in degree or strength; to make something increase in degree or strength
words.exampleEn: Violence intensified during the night.

words.definitionEn: the state or quality of being intense
words.exampleEn: intensity of light/sound/colour

words.definitionEn: involving a lot of work or activity done in a short time
words.exampleEn: an intensive language course

words.definitionEn: what you intend to do
words.exampleEn: She denies possessing the drug with intent to supply.

words.definitionEn: that allows information to be passed continuously and in both directions between a computer or other device and the person who uses it
words.exampleEn: interactive displays/video

words.definitionEn: the act of accepting a gift, an invitation, an offer, etc.
words.exampleEn: Please confirm your acceptance of this offer in writing.

words.definitionEn: the way a computer program presents information to a user or receives information from a user, in particular the layout of the screen and the menus
words.exampleEn: the user interface

words.definitionEn: to get involved in and try to influence a situation that should not really involve you, in a way that annoys other people
words.exampleEn: I wish my mother would stop interfering and let me make my own decisions.

words.definitionEn: the act of getting involved in and trying to influence a situation that should not really involve you, in a way that annoys other people
words.exampleEn: interference (in something), They resent foreign interference in the internal affairs of their country.

words.definitionEn: intended to last for only a short time until somebody/something more permanent is found
words.exampleEn: an interim government/measure/report

words.definitionEn: connected with the inside part of something
words.exampleEn: interior walls

words.definitionEn: the inside part of something
words.exampleEn: the interior of a building/a car

words.definitionEn: located between two places, things, states, etc.
words.exampleEn: an intermediate stage/step in a process

words.definitionEn: to become involved in a situation in order to improve or help it
words.exampleEn: She might have been killed if the neighbours hadn't intervened.

words.definitionEn: action taken to improve or help a situation
words.exampleEn: calls for government intervention to save the steel industry

words.definitionEn: having a close and friendly relationship
words.exampleEn: intimate friends

words.definitionEn: to choose somebody for a job or position of responsibility
words.exampleEn: appoint somebody, They have appointed a new head teacher at my son's school.

words.definitionEn: very interesting because of being unusual or not having an obvious answer
words.exampleEn: These discoveries raise intriguing questions.

words.definitionEn: a person who examines a situation such as an accident or a crime to find out the truth
words.exampleEn: air safety investigators

words.definitionEn: that cannot be seen
words.exampleEn: a wizard who could make himself invisible

words.definitionEn: to mention or use a law, rule, etc. as a reason for doing something
words.exampleEn: It is unlikely that libel laws will be invoked.

words.definitionEn: the act of taking part in something or dealing with somebody
words.exampleEn: involvement in something, US involvement in European wars

words.definitionEn: showing that you really mean the opposite of what you are saying; expressing irony
words.exampleEn: an ironic comment

words.definitionEn: in a way that shows that you really mean the opposite of what you are saying; in a way that expresses irony
words.exampleEn: He smiled ironically.

words.definitionEn: the funny or strange aspect of a situation that is very different from what you expect; a situation like this
words.exampleEn: It was one of life's little ironies.

words.definitionEn: not important to or connected with a situation
words.exampleEn: totally/completely/largely irrelevant

words.definitionEn: pleasure that you have when you recognize and enjoy the good qualities of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: She shows little appreciation of good music.

words.definitionEn: the act of separating somebody/something; the state of being separate
words.exampleEn: geographical isolation

words.definitionEn: connected with a court, a judge or legal judgement
words.exampleEn: judicial powers

words.definitionEn: the place where two or more roads or railway lines meet
words.exampleEn: It was near the junction of City Road and Old Street.

words.definitionEn: the authority that an official organization has to make legal decisions about somebody/something
words.exampleEn: jurisdiction over somebody/something, The English court had no jurisdiction over the defendants.

words.definitionEn: that most people consider to be morally fair and reasonable
words.exampleEn: a just decision/law/society

words.definitionEn: a good reason why something exists or is done
words.exampleEn: justification for doing something, I can see no possible justification for any further tax increases.

words.definitionEn: to take somebody away illegally and keep them as a prisoner, especially in order to get money or something else for returning them
words.exampleEn: Two businessmen have been kidnapped by terrorists.

words.definitionEn: either of the two organs in the body that remove waste products from the blood and produce urine
words.exampleEn: a kidney infection

words.definitionEn: a country that has a king or queen as head of state (= official leader of the country)
words.exampleEn: the United Kingdom

words.definitionEn: a boy or young man
words.exampleEn: Things have changed since I was a lad.

words.definitionEn: a person or company from whom you rent a room, a house, an office, etc.
words.exampleEn: a buy-to-let landlord (= who buys houses and flats in order to rent them out)

words.definitionEn: not seeming to be based on a reason, system or plan and sometimes seeming unfair
words.exampleEn: The choice of players for the team seemed completely arbitrary.

words.definitionEn: something, such as a large building, that you can see clearly from a distance and that will help you to know where you are
words.exampleEn: The Empire State Building is a familiar landmark on the New York skyline.

words.definitionEn: the top part of your legs that forms a flat surface when you are sitting down
words.exampleEn: on somebody's lap, There's only one seat so you'll have to sit on my lap.
